channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats push back after Musk says Trump agrees to close USAID and workers are kept out
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats have delivered a strong rebuke against the Trump administration’s attempt to gut an agency that provides crucial aid overseas to fund education and fight starvation and disease, calling it illegal, vowing a court fight and lambasting billionaire Elon Musk for wielding so
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, FARNOUSH AMIRI and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S. The decision affects some of the Venezuelans currently living in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New York shields abortion pill prescribers after a doctor was indicted in Louisiana
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York Gov. Kathy Hochul on Monday signed a bill to shield the identities of doctors who prescribe abortion medications, days after a physician in the state was charged with prescribing abortion pills to a pregnant minor in Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico for a month
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum and U.S. President Donald Trump have agreed to put planned tariffs on hold for a month. Trump’s tariffs against Canada and China are still slated to go into effect on Tuesday. Staffers of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: At the regional level, provinces are taking their own actions, with multiple premiers saying they have directed their respective liquor boards to stop selling American alcohol.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missouri restaurant says ICE arrested some employees at work
item.description: "I grew up, like, just right down the road from here. I always come to eat maybe once or twice a week at least," customer Autmn Emery said.
item.dc:creator: Chad Mira, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Even those who have not worked directly in DEI roles but went to the training have been put on administrative leave.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states, including Nevada, upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's threat of tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is aimed at stopping the flow of fentanyl into the U.S. Ingredients for fentanyl are produced in China and used by pharmaceutical companies to make legal painkillers.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Experts explain that and more about FEMA
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Federal disaster aid is nearly everywhere. About 94% of Americans live in a county that has gotten Federal Emergency Management Agency help for disasters since 2011.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pacific Coast Highway reopens nearly a month after devastating Southern California wildfires
item.description: The Pacific Coast Highway is back open in Malibu, California, nearly a month after the deadly Palisades Fire ripped through hillside Los Angeles neighborhoods, destroying properties all the way down to the beach.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The city of Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to a woman who alleged that former policeman Derek Chauvin pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just months before he used the same maneuver on George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Man hit in head by debris from Philadelphia plane crash that broke through diner window
item.description: A Philadelphia diner patron was injured Friday when a piece of debris from a medevac jet that crashed hurtled through a window and hit the man in the head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army identifies 3rd soldier killed in DC crash
item.description: The U.S. Army on Saturday identified the third soldier who died last week during a training mission that ended in a midair crash between an Army Black Hawk helicopter and an American Airlines regional jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Officials say remains of 55 of 67 victims of midair collision have been recovered and identified
item.description: Families of victims of the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001 visited the crash site on Sunday and divers scoured the submerged wreckage for more remains after authorities said they've recovered and identified 55 of the 67 people killed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump warns 'abusive' countries with midnight tariff deadline and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Orleans sued over Bourbon Street terror attack, accused of negligence that cost lives
item.description: New Orleans terrorist attack victims and survivors have filed a civil lawsuit against city leaders for negligence after Shamsud-Din Jabbar drove his truck into Bourbon Street crowds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arkansas nurse charged with using dead patient's name in attempt to purchase hundreds of oxycodone pills
item.description: A nurse in Arkansas is facing felony charges after investigators say she used a dead man's name when attempting to purchase oxycodone pills at pharmacies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 55 victims in Washington, DC midair collision pulled from Potomac River as recovery operation continues
item.description: The bodies of 55 victims have been pulled from the Potomac River in Washington, D.C., following the midair collision between a commercial jet and a Black Hark helicopter on Jan. 29.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Black box recovered in Philadelphia medical transport jet crash: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder was located at the site of initial impact, at a depth of 8 feet, according to the NTSB.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, WGN 9

item.title: NTSB releases video of responders searching through eerie site of fatal Philadelphia Learjet crash
item.description: The NTSB published video showing officials at the site of the fatal Jan. 31 plane crash in Philadelphia, which killed at least seven people and injured 22 others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter wins posthumous Grammy for narrating an audiobook of his Sunday school lessons
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former President Jimmy Carter has won a posthumous Grammy award. Carter, the peanut farmer who won the presidency in the wake of the Watergate scandal and Vietnam War, died in December at age 100.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Watch: Fans boo US national anthem at NHL game in Canada amid trade war
item.description: The booing came shortly after Trump signed an order imposing stiff tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ICE nabs 7 illegal immigrants during Philadelphia car wash raid
item.description: Agents with U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Philadelphia announced that an operation led to the arrest of seven illegal immigrants during a car wash raid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID security leaders on leave after trying to keep Musk's DOGE from classified info: officials
item.description: The two USAID security officials believed themselves legally obligated to deny access to Musk's DOGE crew since it lacked high-enough security clearance to access the information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Iowa honors Caitlin Clark by retiring her number and hanging it in the rafters
item.description: Clark returned to Iowa’s Carver-Hawkeye Arena on Sunday to have her number 22 retired in a ceremony after the Hawkeyes’ 76-67 win over No. 4 USC, one season after she completed her historic college career.
item.dc:creator: JOHN BOHNENKAMP, Associated Press, Fox 5
